understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each KA59 level by positioning the selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked '+' in the semantic grid was occupied by a selectable at the same time, progressing through all levels until a WIN state ended the run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used MOVE_* actions to shift only the currently selected piece one grid step (3-pixel cell) in the chosen direction, with collisions affected by nearby objects: walls generally blocked motion, while pushable blocks could be displaced if adjacent in the movement direction and space allowed. Multiple selectable pieces existed, and control could be switched at any time via CLICK on a selectable’s exact pixel/grid position (the same coordinate system as player.position and objects), changing which piece responded to subsequent movement inputs; the movement.*_blocked flags were advisory hints about adjacency rather than guaranteed failure.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether the adjacent wall actually blocks movement or if there's a wrap/clip behavior when moving into it.
history.reasoning: Test whether the adjacent wall actually blocks movement or if there's a wrap/clip behavior when moving into it.

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_UP | Test whether the surrounding wall is truly impassable or if moving into it causes a glitch/shift that can free the piece toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Test whether the surrounding wall is truly impassable or if moving into it causes a glitch/shift that can free the piece toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | Although the heuristic says blocked by a wall, earlier moves succeeded despite walls; try moving right again to test if walls allow passage/teleport along this boundary toward the goals.
history.reasoning: Although the heuristic says blocked by a wall, earlier moves succeeded despite walls; try moving right again to test if walls allow passage/teleport along this boundary toward the goals.

history.summary: Turn 4: MOVE_DOWN | Try moving despite the wall-blocked hint; we likely need to go around the central wall by heading downward first.
history.reasoning: Try moving despite the wall-blocked hint; we likely need to go around the central wall by heading downward first.

history.summary: Turn 5: CLICK | Switch control to the other selectable to see if it has a possible route out toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Switch control to the other selectable to see if it has a possible route out toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 6: CLICK | This piece is fully surrounded by walls, so switch to the other selectable and try moving it toward the goal cluster on the right.
history.reasoning: This piece is fully surrounded by walls, so switch to the other selectable and try moving it toward the goal cluster on the right.

history.summary: Turn 7: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether the adjacent wall actually blocks movement; if it yields, we can advance toward the goal area on the right.
history.reasoning: Test whether the adjacent wall actually blocks movement; if it yields, we can advance toward the goal area on the right.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_RIGHT | Only possible interaction is to the right where the other selectable is; try pushing to see if it can move or trigger a swap/clip past the wall toward the goals.
history.reasoning: Only possible interaction is to the right where the other selectable is; try pushing to see if it can move or trigger a swap/clip past the wall toward the goals.

history.summary: Turn 9: CLICK | Switch control to the other selectable near the goal so we can move it onto the + area.
history.reasoning: Switch control to the other selectable near the goal so we can move it onto the + area.

history.summary: Turn 10: CLICK | Switch control to the other selectable so we can try moving it toward the goal area on the right.
history.reasoning: Switch control to the other selectable so we can try moving it toward the goal area on the right.

history.summary: Turn 11: CLICK | Switch control to the other selectable near the goal so we can position it onto the + area.
history.reasoning: Switch control to the other selectable near the goal so we can position it onto the + area.

history.summary: Turn 12: MOVE_RIGHT | We are adjacent to the goal tiles; try stepping right onto the goal area (goal is non-collidable) to trigger WIN.
history.reasoning: We are adjacent to the goal tiles; try stepping right onto the goal area (goal is non-collidable) to trigger WIN.

history.summary: Turn 13: MOVE_UP | Try moving up onto the goal area (goals are non-collidable, so this may be allowed despite the wall hint).
history.reasoning: Try moving up onto the goal area (goals are non-collidable, so this may be allowed despite the wall hint).
